CREATE TABLE test_data (y FLOAT, x FLOAT);
INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (1.5, 2.5), (NULL, 3.5), (4.5, NULL), (NULL, NULL), (5.0, 6.0);
SELECT regr_count(y, x) FROM test_data WHERE y IS NOT NULL;
SELECT regr_count(y, x) FROM (SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE x IS NOT NULL) AS subquery;
CREATE TABLE other_data (y FLOAT, x FLOAT);
SELECT regr_count(t1.y, t1.x) FROM test_data AS t1 JOIN other_data AS t2 ON t1.x = t2.x;
SELECT regr_count(y, x) FROM (SELECT y, x FROM test_data WHERE y > 1) AS filtered WHERE x < 5;
SELECT regr_count(y, x) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT y, x FROM test_data) AS unique_pairs;
SELECT regr_count(t1.y, t1.x) FROM test_data AS t1 INNER JOIN other_data AS t2 ON t1.y = t2.y WHERE t1.x > 2;
